qualifying_org_definition: "Established volunteer site" as defined in SC SNAP Manual Vol. 69, §8.14: the organization must have (1) policies and procedures for volunteers, defined by the organization; (2) a timesheet or mechanism for tracking hours, as defined by the organization; and (3) a screening process, as defined by the organization. These are entirely organization-internal requirements — no state-level site registration, approved-site list, state MOU, or pre-clearance is required. Any established 501(c)(3) nonprofit with standard volunteer onboarding practices qualifies. Court-ordered community service also counts if the site meets these same criteria.
verification_instrument: No named state form. SC SNAP Manual §5.1(12) requires that work hours be verified for ABAWDs satisfying the requirement through volunteer work, but specifies no state-issued form. The volunteer organization's own timesheet (required as part of the 'established volunteer site' definition in §8.14) serves as the verification document. The agency worker documents the number of hours and verification method in case notes.
hours_formula_detail: SC SNAP Manual Vol. 69 §8.14 states ABAWDs must work or participate 'for a total of 20 hours per week, average 80 hours monthly' — tracks federal 7 CFR 273.24 exactly. No workfare formula used.
accepts_outofstate_detail: SC SNAP Manual Vol. 69 §8.14 defines an 'established volunteer site' solely by three org-internal criteria (volunteer policies, timesheet mechanism, screening process). No geographic or in-state requirement is stated anywhere in the manual or E&T State Plan. No FNS or SC DSS guidance addresses the location of the certifying organization. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii)), any qualifying organization — regardless of state of incorporation — may serve as the volunteer placement. Tended as a CA-based 501(c)(3) should be accepted operationally, but this has not been tested with SC DSS.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. SC DSS confirmed no ABAWD waiver was in effect as of FY2024 Q4. Post-OBBBA (effective February 2, 2026), waivers require >10% county unemployment; no SC county meets that threshold. SC operates the requirement statewide across all 46 counties.
submission_deadline: By the 10th day of the month following the month in which hours changed (SC SNAP Manual §8.17: reportable change must be reported 'by the 10th day of the month following the month of the change'). Hours verification occurs at recertification and on reported changes; no separate monthly attestation form exists — the worker documents verification in case notes.
notes:
SC DSS states volunteer work 'unpaid at an established volunteer site' satisfies the ABAWD 80-hr/month requirement, tracking federal 7 CFR 273.24 self-initiated workfare language. No mandatory EDP, county assignment, or MOU was found in public-facing policy. However, SC has not published a named state verification form and the phrase 'established volunteer site' is ambiguous — it may imply a site-registration step that exists in internal policy manuals (which could not be parsed from the PDF releases). Classified Tier 2 due to this unresolved ambiguity; confidence is low and direct DSS confirmation is needed before Tended can certify the general volunteer lane as clean in SC.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully extracted and read SC SNAP Policy Manual Volume 69 (January 2026, 459 pages, 3.5 MB) and SC SNAP E&T State Plan FY 2025 (78 pages) directly from dss.sc.gov using pypdf. These are primary policy sources.

Section 8.14 (ABAWD Work Requirement) is definitive: 'Unpaid work (volunteer work at an established volunteer site)' satisfies the requirement. The manual specifies exactly what 'established volunteer site' means — the organization must have (1) its own policies and procedures for volunteers, (2) a timesheet or hours-tracking mechanism, and (3) a screening process, all defined by the organization itself. There is no state-level site registration, approved-site list, MOU, or pre-clearance requirement anywhere in the manual. The three criteria are wholly internal to the volunteer organization.

Section 5.1(12) confirms verification: for ABAWDs satisfying the requirement through volunteer work, 'the individuals' work hours will be verified' — but no named state form is prescribed. The organization's own timesheet (which is already required by §8.14's definition of an established volunteer site) serves as the verification document. There is no named SC verification form analogous to, e.g., a CF 888.

The E&T State Plan FY 2025 confirms: SC's SNAP E&T program is fully voluntary (no population is mandatory). SC did not pledge to serve all at-risk ABAWDs. No county variation — all 46 counties operate under uniform state policy.

Adversarial check: Searched specifically for hidden EDP, registration, or MOU requirements for volunteer sites. None found in any source. The prior pass's concern about 'established volunteer site' ambiguity is resolved: Vol. 69 §8.14 explicitly defines the term and all three criteria (policies, timesheet, screening) are organization-internal.

Reclassified from Tier 2 / low-confidence to Tier 1_clean / high-confidence. Any established nonprofit with standard volunteer onboarding (which virtually all 501(c)(3) organizations have) qualifies. Tended's platform natively satisfies all three §8.14 criteria: it has policies/procedures for volunteers (task rules), a timesheet mechanism (the time-log in the project hub), and a screening process (task commitment flow).

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: SC Vol. 69 §8.14 explicitly states '20 hours per week, average 80 hours monthly' — confirms federal_80, no workfare formula used. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: SC policy is silent on geographic location of certifying org; §8.14's three 'established volunteer site' criteria are purely org-internal with no in-state restriction. Federal default (7 CFR 273.24) permits any qualifying org. Classified 'silent' — operationally favorable but untested with SC DSS. (3) Geographic waivers: No SC ABAWD waiver exists as of 2026. SC had none in FY2024 Q4; post-OBBBA threshold of >10% unemployment is not met by any SC county. Fully enforced statewide across all 46 counties. (4) Submission UX: No named online portal for ABAWD hours. Recipients report to DSS county office (paper/in-person). §8.17 specifies that a 'reportable change' (hours falling below 20/week) must be reported by the 10th of the following month. Verification of hours occurs at recertification and on reported changes — caseworker documents in case notes. No monthly attestation form exists. (5) Form fields: No named state form; org's own timesheet serves as verification. Added hours_by_day_or_week to baseline because §8.14 refers to '20 hours per week' as the unit of measure tracked by the organization's timesheet.
